response: There are many scenarios to consider when calculating this probability. The week contains five school days. Karen packs ham sandwiches on three of these days. She packs cake on exactly one day. For the ham and cake to appear together, the single cake day must fall on one of the three days designated for ham. The probability of the cake landing on a ham day is three out of five. This fraction converts to sixty percent. Therefore the chance that Karen packs a ham sandwich and cake on the same day is sixty percent.
